failure_surface: All target columns (G, H, I) received identical VLOOKUP/INDEX-MATCH formulas returning the same data from Sheet3, rather than retrieving different columns as intended
root_cause: Agent applied a static INDEX column parameter ('2') across all output cells without varying the column index based on which target column (G vs H vs I) needed to receive different data from the source sheet
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When populating multiple adjacent columns with related lookups, vary the INDEX/MATCH column parameter proportionally to the output column's offset from the first target column
evidence: All cells G2:I7 contain identical formula: =IFERROR(INDEX(Sheet3!$B$2:$D$300,MATCH(TRIM(F{row}),Sheet3!$A$2:$A$300,0),2),"No match")
Column parameter '2' in INDEX function was hardcoded and not adjusted per target column G, H, or I
Agent iterated through columns ['G', 'H', 'I'] but did not use loop variable to compute corresponding source column offset

memory_items.title: Multi-Column Lookup Formula Variation
memory_items.lesson: When writing lookup formulas across multiple adjacent target columns, each column typically requires a different returned column index from the source range
memory_items.generalization: Calculate the INDEX column parameter dynamically based on target column offset rather than hardcoding a fixed value

memory_items.title: Formula Parameter Validation Across Iterations
memory_items.lesson: When generating similar formulas in a loop over columns or rows, verify that any numeric parameters vary appropriately to produce differentiated results
memory_items.generalization: Add sanity checks confirming formula outputs differ when expected before finalizing batch operations

memory_items.title: TRIM() Integration in Cross-Sheet Lookups
memory_items.lesson: When matching data between sheets where whitespace inconsistencies exist, TRIM() should be applied to both the lookup value and lookup array for reliable matches
memory_items.generalization: Use MATCH with TRIM wrapper when source and reference data may have inconsistent whitespace formatting

edits.content:
### Multi-Column Formula Generation
When populating adjacent target columns with lookup formulas:
- Each output column typically maps to a different source column in the lookup range
- Dynamically calculate the INDEX column parameter based on target column offset: use `(target_col_idx - start_col_idx + 1)` instead of hardcoding
- Verify that iterating over multiple columns produces differentiated results before saving
- Example pattern: for columns G(7), H(8), I(9) referencing source range starting at B(2), use column_offset = (current_col_num - 7 + 1)
